hero.headline: 3.1 Million Contracts. One Question:
hero.sub: RUBLI tracks every Mexican federal procurement contract since 2002. Powered by ML risk scoring, network analysis, and 748 documented corruption cases.
hero.heroSubtitle: Mexican federal procurement data analyzed by machine learning to detect corruption patterns across 12 government sectors
hero.riskDisclaimer: Risk scores indicate statistical similarity to documented corruption patterns — not proof of wrongdoing.
hero.storySubtitle: RUBLI analyzes 3.1 million federal procurement contracts using machine learning to surface corruption patterns, ghost companies, and market manipulation.
hero.whatIs: The largest open analysis of Mexican federal procurement. 23 years of contract data. One risk model. Every score explained.
hero.credibility: Source: COMPRANET 2002-2025  |  Model v6.5  |  AUC 0.828  |  Updated March 2026

ctaPanels.headline: Start your investigation
ctaPanels.sub: Choose where to begin — every entry point leads to evidence.

cases.title: Investigate a case
cases.desc: 25 documented corruption cases with linked contracts and vendors

administrations.title: Explore by administration
administrations.desc: Compare risk patterns across presidential terms from 2000 to today

vendors.title: Find a vendor
vendors.desc: Search 320K vendors, see risk scores, SHAP explanations, and network ties

sectors.title: View the risk map
sectors.desc: 12 sectors ranked by risk rate — from health to defense

how.title: From Raw Data to Investigation Leads
how.sub: Three steps. 9 active ML features. 12 per-sector models.
how.step1_body: 23 years of COMPRANET procurement data -- 3.1 million contracts, 320K vendors, 12 sectors. Cleaned, classified, and normalized.
how.step2_body: ML risk scoring with 9 active z-score features, per-sector logistic models, PU-learning correction. Train AUC 0.798, Test AUC 0.828.
how.step3_body: Ranked investigation queue with evidence, SHAP explanations, confidence intervals. Every score has a reason.
how.label: HOW WE DETECT PATTERNS
how.headline: From raw data to risk intelligence
how.step01Body: 3.1M contracts from COMPRANET (2002-2025), cleaned and standardized
how.step02Body: Vendor concentration, atypical prices, direct awards, network patterns...
how.step03Body: Compared against 289 documented corruption cases across all 12 sectors
how.step04Body: We grade procurement health from A to F for every sector
how.disclaimer: Scores are statistical risk indicators, not proof of corruption. A high score means the contract resembles documented corruption patterns.

reportCard.label: OUR NATIONAL GRADE
reportCard.headline: How healthy is Mexican public procurement?

aria.label: AUTOMATED INVESTIGATION
aria.headline: Intelligence Briefing
aria.body: Our 9-module Automated Risk Investigation Algorithm classifies 198K vendors into investigation tiers, detects monopoly, ghost company, and institutional capture patterns, and cross-references against SAT, SFP, and ASF registries.

cta.title: Open-source. No account required.
cta.sub: Data from COMPRANET 2002-2025. Model v6.5. AUC 0.828.
cta.headline: Start investigating
cta.body: Browse contracts, investigate vendors, and discover risk patterns.
cta.redThreadTeaser: Follow the investigation trail on Laboratorios Pisa

featuredCase.label: DOCUMENTED CASE
featuredCase.description: This pharmaceutical company received government contracts with {{directAwardPct}}% direct awards. The risk model assigns a score of {{riskScore}} — the highest possible — indicating its contracting pattern almost perfectly matches documented fraud cases.
